label: Heroku Postgres
description: Connect to a Heroku-managed PostgreSQL add-on using your app's DATABASE_URL connection string.

form.title: Connect Heroku Postgres
form.subtitle: Paste your DATABASE_URL from the Heroku Dashboard to connect Anton to your Heroku Postgres add-on.

methods.label: DATABASE_URL Connection String
methods.description: Paste the full postgres:// connection string from your Heroku app's Postgres add-on credentials. The simplest and fastest way to connect — no manual host/port entry needed.
methods.how_to:
## Find your DATABASE_URL in Heroku Dashboard

1. Open [Heroku Dashboard](https://dashboard.heroku.com) and select your app.
2. Click the **Resources** tab.
3. Under **Add-ons**, click **Heroku Postgres** to open the add-on page.
4. Click the **Settings** tab on the Heroku Postgres page.
5. Click **View Credentials**. Heroku will show the full connection string under **URI**.
6. Copy the entire string — it starts with `postgres://` or `postgresql://`.
7. Paste it into the **DATABASE_URL** field below.

## Notes

- SSL is required for all Heroku Postgres connections. Anton enforces `sslmode=require` automatically if not present in the URL.
- Credentials can rotate after certain Heroku maintenance events. If the connection fails, revisit **View Credentials** to get a fresh URL.
- The URI contains your username, password, host, port, and database name all in one — keep it secret.
- Free-tier (Hobby) databases are deprecated on Heroku; ensure your plan is active.

fields.name: database_url
fields.label: DATABASE_URL
fields.description: Full connection URI from Heroku Dashboard → Resources → Heroku Postgres → Settings → View Credentials → URI.

fields.name: ssl_mode
fields.label: SSL Mode
fields.description: Heroku Postgres requires SSL. Leave as 'require' unless your plan mandates stricter verification.

methods.label: Manual Credentials
methods.description: Enter the host, port, database name, username, and password individually. Use this if you prefer not to share the full connection string or need to specify fields separately.
methods.how_to:
## Enter credentials manually

1. Open [Heroku Dashboard](https://dashboard.heroku.com) and select your app.
2. Click the **Resources** tab.
3. Under **Add-ons**, click **Heroku Postgres** to open the add-on page.
4. Click the **Settings** tab, then **View Credentials**.
5. Copy the individual values — **Host**, **Database**, **User**, **Password**, and **Port** — into the fields below.

## Notes

- The default Heroku Postgres port is **5432**.
- SSL is mandatory; Anton will set `sslmode=require` for all connections.
- Credentials shown in the dashboard may change after Heroku maintenance or a manual credential rotation. Update these fields if the connection drops unexpectedly.

fields.name: host
fields.label: Host
fields.description: The server hostname from Heroku Postgres credentials (e.g. ec2-xx-xx-xx-xx.compute-1.amazonaws.com).

fields.name: database
fields.label: Database Name
fields.description: The database name from your Heroku Postgres credentials.

fields.name: username
fields.label: Username
fields.description: The user/role from your Heroku Postgres credentials.

fields.name: password
fields.label: Password
fields.description: The password from your Heroku Postgres credentials. Keep this secret.

fields.name: ssl_mode
fields.label: SSL Mode
fields.description: Heroku Postgres requires SSL. Leave as 'require' unless your plan mandates stricter verification.
